claims.claim_text: Quantization represents model weights with lower-precision data types, reducing memory/computational cost while introducing efficiency-versus-quality tradeoffs.
claims.span_hint: section: Start With The Problem Quantization Solves
claims.rationale: The Hugging Face quantization concept guide directly explains lower-precision representation, smaller memory footprint, faster inference, and the accuracy tradeoff.

claims.claim_text: GGUF is a model file format used for inference with GGML/llama.cpp-style workflows and supports quantized model storage for local inference.
claims.span_hint: sections: Quantization Is Not The Same As File Format; Why GGUF Matters
claims.rationale: The GGUF documentation directly describes GGUF as an inference-oriented format for GGML and notes support for many quantized data types.

claims.claim_text: 4-bit quantization is often what makes larger models feasible to load and run on smaller local hardware.
claims.rationale: The Transformers quantization docs explicitly tie 4-bit and 8-bit quantization to loading larger models that otherwise would not fit in memory.

claims.claim_text: Quantized open-model ecosystems grew quickly because 4-bit quantization made local use practical.
claims.anchor_text: It is also why quantized open-model ecosystems grew so quickly.
claims.suggested_rewrite: It is also why quantized model variants matter so much in local-inference workflows.
claims.rationale: This is a broad causal ecosystem-history claim without a bounded primary source and is not necessary to preserve the teaching point.

further_reading.title: Quantization concepts
further_reading.why_relevant: Good primary background on what quantization changes in memory, speed, and accuracy terms.

further_reading.title: GGUF
further_reading.why_relevant: Explains what GGUF is, what it stores, and how it relates to quantized local inference workflows.

further_reading.title: QLoRA: Efficient Finetuning of Quantized LLMs
further_reading.why_relevant: Useful deeper reading on why 4-bit quantization became important for making larger models practical on constrained hardware.

notes: Most remaining content is pedagogical framing, learner guidance, quiz material, or exercises and does not need inline citations at kubernetes.io/docs-style density.
